name: Bara Brith (Speckled Bread)
recipeInstructions:
This is Wales' traditional rich fruit bread. South Wales makes it with baking powder; Northerners prefer yeast as the raising agent. Either way it's delicious.
Soak the dried fruit and sugar overnight in the tea. You can use either fresh tea, or the cold dregs from the teapot (this gives a good strong colour).
Next day, sift the flour into the mixture and fold it in gently.
Add the lightly beaten egg and mix well.
Line a small loaf tin with buttered paper and pour in the mixture, smoothing it evenly.
Bake in a gentle oven at 300°F (150°C) gas mark 2 for 1.5 hours.
Cool completely before storing for at least 2 days in the tin to mature moist and rich.
Traditionalists say you should never butter the Bara Brith, but Dorothy says to do so as it's lovely that way.
